5347b438.summary: OpenWrt system process manager init.d script API. Provides standard functions for service management, process supervisor configuration, and jail/sandbox settings within /etc/init.d/ scripts.
5347b438.when_to_use: Use when writing or modifying OpenWrt init scripts to manage daemon lifecycles, service triggers, and process isolation.

15c8ec11.summary: Native filesystem access module for ucode. Implements robust, low-level POSIX-style operations including atomic file writes, directory traversal, file stat, and symbolic link management.
15c8ec11.when_to_use: Use for all ucode-based filesystem interactions on OpenWrt, especially when atomicity or precise permission control is required.

8276580d.summary: ucode bindings for the OpenWrt uloop event loop. Enables asynchronous programming, recursive timers, network socket monitoring, and child process management within ucode scripts.
8276580d.when_to_use: Essential for non-blocking ucode applications, periodic background tasks, and event-driven network programming.

3412fda2.summary: Unified Configuration Interface (UCI) bindings for ucode. Provides a comprehensive API for reading, writing, and applying OpenWrt system configurations directly from ucode scripts.
3412fda2.when_to_use: Use to programmatically manage system settings, parse config sections, and integrate custom services with the OpenWrt UCI ecosystem.

76f8e711.summary: UCI default configuration schema for networking and QoS services. Defines the standard structure for interface-to-class mapping and bandwidth prioritization rules on OpenWrt.
76f8e711.when_to_use: Refer to this schema when configuring traffic shaping or quality-of-service parameters to ensure compatibility with the system defaults.

mock-hash-xhr-456.summary: LuCI JavaScript AJAX utility (XHR). Provides a legacy but widely used wrapper for performing asynchronous HTTP requests within LuCI web interfaces.
mock-hash-xhr-456.when_to_use: Use when maintaining existing LuCI views that require data fetching without full page reloads.
